statement: A converged Daedalus workshop terminal is 'submit' — it permits SUBMISSION of the candidate to TELOS authorization, never authorization itself. Convergence is not authorization; two real refusals (authz-004 on v11, authz-007 on v14) are the preserved precedent.

Objection identity is ALWAYS controller-recomputed (sha256 over {scope, claim, sorted evidence_refs}); a model-supplied objection_hash is never trusted, and dispositions are matched against the recomputed key, so a seat can forge neither an objection nor its reference.

ONLY an explicit validated resolution / supersession / withdrawal record changes an objection's status — a silently-dropped objection stays open and blocks convergence. Only the ORIGINATING seat may retire its own objection, and the disposing response must carry real provenance.

oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-daedalus.mjs (silently-dropped objection blocks convergence; not-originating-seat and no-provenance dispositions rejected)

statement: Every seat call in a converging round carries a real provenance key (provider:response_id; placeholders rejected), and keys are pairwise distinct — serial rounds with invalid/shared provenance are burned (cannot converge), and in parallel authorship all FIVE calls (2 authors, integrator, 2 verifiers) must be distinct so no response id is replayed across calls.

oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-daedalus.mjs (invalid-provenance round discarded; provenance-reused-across-calls cannot converge)

statement: Parallel convergence requires the integration matrix to be field-complete AND a strict BIJECTION over the constraints-declared obligation id set — every declared obligation mapped by exactly one row, none dropped, none invented, none duplicated. The obligation set is content-committed into the constraints source node, so it is not caller-mutable.

oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-daedalus.mjs (coverage bijection: missing/extra/duplicate ids all refuse convergence)

statement: A verifier reporting 'violated' or any non-empty conflict list is an explicit conflict routed to The Eye (terminal 'needs-eye') — never silently blended. A missing or unrecognized verdict cannot converge either; it is not read as tacit approval.
An explicit dossier.authorship === 'parallel' request with no callParallelSeat adapter injected BLOCKS with PARALLEL_AUTHORSHIP_UNAVAILABLE rather than silently downgrading to the serial loop.
